creator: tobez@tobez.org
text:
On Sat, Dec 22, 2001 at 07:00:04AM -0800, Miguel Mendez wrote:
> The following reply was made to PR ports/33090; it has been noted by GNATS.
> 
> From: Miguel Mendez <flynn@energyhq.homeip.net>
> To: freebsd-gnats-submit@FreeBSD.org, flynn@energyhq.homeip.net
> Cc:  
> Subject: Re: ports/33090: lang/perl5 is un-fetchable
> Date: Sat, 22 Dec 2001 15:53:22 +0100
> 
>  It seems ok, sorry, just that only 1 site works for the two files....

More precisely, some sites work for perl-5.6.1.tar.gz, and other sites
work for BSDPAN-5.6.1.tar.gz, unless you use MASTER_SITE_OVERRIDE, in
which case both work fine.

Please see comments in lang/perl5/Makefile.

Someone told me that the ability to specify master sites for particular
distfiles will be introduced to the ports collection soon.  When this
happens, I'll use this new mechanism to make the fetching of this port
distfiles less annoying.

Cheers,
=Anton.
